name: DIFFUSE AXONAL INJURY

demographics.Incidence: most common type of primary traumatic injury in patients with severe head trauma
demographics.Path: much of the injury is microscopic
demographics.Histo: multiple axonal retraction balls (HALLMARK), numerous perivascular hemorrhages

dominant_finding: 20% of lesions with small central areas of petechial hemorrhage
distribution: 20% of lesions with small central areas of petechial hemorrhage
discriminating_features: 20% of lesions with small central areas of petechial hemorrhage
foci of decreased density (usually seen when > 1.5 cm in size)
multiple small oval / round foci of decreased signal intensity on T1WI + increased signal

general: 20% of lesions with small central areas of petechial hemorrhage
foci of decreased density (usually seen when > 1.5 cm in size)
multiple small oval / round foci of decreased signal intensity on T1WI + increased signal
